overrides.override_summary: No waiver of disclaimer of reliance — no questionnaire or acknowledgment may waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to rely on pre-sale representations despite integration clauses

overrides.override_summary: Added disclosure about California Fast Food Act (Cal. Lab. Code §§ 1474-1475) requiring compliance with health, safety, and employment standards including minimum wages. No franchisor training or fee provided for compliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees face additional labor cost burden (higher minimum wages for fast food workers) not reflected in Item 7 or Item 19 data

overrides.override_summary: Any release required at renewal or transfer will not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain right to sue under state franchise law even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law but franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's ability to terminate franchise due to bankruptcy filing

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia forum selection for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland to the extent required by Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland law may override Georgia choice of law for franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Sets specific limitation period for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days' notice of non-renewal required under Minn. Stat. § 80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer cure and notice periods than standard FA provisions (which provide as little as 24 hours to 30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Consent to transfer will not be unreasonably withheld
overrides.why_it_matters: Strengthens franchisee's transfer rights beyond FA requirements

overrides.override_summary: Any release at renewal/transfer will not apply to extent prohibited by Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee claims under Minnesota franchise law despite release requirement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor may not require litigation outside Minnesota, waiver of jury trial, or consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes under Minn. Stat. § 80C.21 and Rule 2860.4400(J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Nullifies Georgia forum selection, jury trial waiver, and liquidated damages provisions for MN franchisees — among the strongest state franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provision might not be enforceable under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Potentially eliminates up to $180K+ in liquidated damages exposure for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer will not apply to claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects ND franchisee claims despite release requirement

overrides.override_summary: Covenants not to compete generally considered unenforceable in North Dakota; franchisor will seek to enforce to extent enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete effectively unenforceable in ND — franchisee can open competing business immediately after termination

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in North Dakota to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia forum selection for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies (except federal law)
overrides.why_it_matters: Replaces Georgia choice of law entirely for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance with WA AG — agreed to remove no-poach/no-hire provisions from franchise agreements, not enforce existing provisions, and notify franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees have full freedom to hire from other JJ locations; no-poach provisions unenforceable
